questions.q: Given a cover $\mathcal{U}=\{U_\alpha\}$ of $X$ and a sheaf $\mathcal{F}$, a $1$-cocycle is a family $\{t_{\alpha\beta}\in\mathcal{F}(U_{\alpha\beta})\}$ satisfying which identity on $U_{\alpha\beta\gamma}$?
questions.explain: The alternating Čech differential gives $(d t)_{\alpha\beta\gamma}=t_{\beta\gamma}-t_{\alpha\gamma}+t_{\alpha\beta}$; setting it to zero is the cocycle condition on triple overlaps.

choices: Because $\mathcal{F}$ is a constant sheaf on $X$.
Because a $0$-cocycle is a family of local sections agreeing on overlaps, which is a global section of $\mathcal{F}$.
questions.explain: A $0$-cochain is $\{s_\alpha\in\mathcal{F}(U_\alpha)\}$; $(ds)_{\alpha\beta}=s_\beta-s_\alpha=0$ forces the $s_\alpha$ to glue by the sheaf axiom, yielding exactly a global section.

questions.q: For the circle $S^1$ with the constant sheaf $\underline{\mathbb{Z}}$ and a standard good cover by two overlapping arcs whose intersection has two connected components, compute $\dim_{\mathbb{Q}} (\check{H}^1(S^1,\underline{\mathbb{Z}})\otimes\mathbb{Q})$.
questions.explain: $\check{H}^1(S^1,\underline{\mathbb{Z}})\cong\mathbb{Z}$ (the winding-number class), so rationally it is $1$-dimensional.

questions.q: A cover $\mathcal{U} = \{U_\alpha\}$ of $X$ is Leray for a sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ when:
choices: $H^i(U_{\alpha_0\cdots\alpha_p},\mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $i > 0$ and every finite intersection
questions.explain: The Leray condition asks that $\mathcal{F}$ be acyclic on every finite intersection of the cover. When satisfied, the canonical map $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},\mathcal{F}) \to H^p(X,\mathcal{F})$ is an isomorphism for every $p$.

questions.q: Which of the following guarantees that an affine open cover of a scheme is Leray for any quasi-coherent sheaf?
choices: Serre's theorem: $H^i(\mathrm{Spec}\,R, \widetilde M) = 0$ for $i > 0$ and every $R$-module $M$
questions.explain: Serre proved that affine schemes have no higher quasi-coherent cohomology, so every finite intersection of affine opens (also affine when the scheme is separated) is $\mathcal{F}$-acyclic. Hence an affine open cover is Leray.

questions.q: The standard affine cover of $\mathbb{P}^2$ has three charts $U_0,U_1,U_2$. How many terms does the resulting Čech complex $\check C^0 \to \check C^1 \to \check C^2$ have in $\check C^1$?
questions.explain: $\check C^1$ indexes strictly-increasing pairs $(\alpha_0,\alpha_1)$ from $\{0,1,2\}$, so its rank is $\binom{3}{2} = 3$. (Triple intersections contribute $\binom{3}{3}=1$ term in $\check C^2$.)

questions.q: A short exact sequence of sheaves $0\to\mathcal{F}'\to\mathcal{F}\to\mathcal{F}''\to 0$ produces a long exact sequence whose connecting homomorphism $\delta$ goes in which direction?
choices: $\delta$ goes between $\mathcal{F}$ and $\mathcal{F}''$ at the same degree
questions.explain: The connecting map raises degree by one and jumps from the quotient sheaf back to the subsheaf: $\delta\colon H^p(\mathcal{F}'') \to H^{p+1}(\mathcal{F}')$. In Čech language it lifts a local section of $\mathcal{F}''$ to one of $\mathcal{F}$; the failure of the lifts to glue is a class in $H^{p+1}(\mathcal{F}')$.

questions.q: In the exponential sequence $0 \to \underline{\mathbb{Z}} \to \mathcal{O} \to \mathcal{O}^\times \to 0$ on $\mathbb{C}^\times$, what does $\delta\colon H^0(\mathcal{O}^\times) \to H^1(\underline{\mathbb{Z}})$ send the global section $z$ to?
choices: the winding number class, which generates $H^1(\mathbb{C}^\times,\underline{\mathbb{Z}}) = \mathbb{Z}$
questions.explain: On $\mathbb{C}^\times$ the global section $z$ has no single-valued logarithm, so its image under $\delta$ is non-trivial. Concretely the class is $1\in H^1(\mathbb{C}^\times,\underline{\mathbb{Z}}) = \mathbb{Z}$, the winding-number generator.

questions.q: From $0\to\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}^1}(-1)\to\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}^1}\to\mathcal{O}_p\to 0$ (skyscraper at a point) and the vanishing $H^i(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}^1}(-1))=0$ for all $i$, deduce $\dim H^0(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}_p)$.
questions.explain: The sandwiching $H^i(\mathcal{O}(-1)) = 0$ forces $H^0(\mathcal{O}) \xrightarrow{\sim} H^0(\mathcal{O}_p)$. Since $\dim H^0(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}) = 1$, we get $\dim H^0(\mathcal{O}_p) = 1$ — the stalk at $p$.

questions.q: Which property of the global-sections functor $\Gamma(X,-)$ is measured by the derived functors $R^i\Gamma$?
questions.explain: $\Gamma(X,-)$ is left exact but not right exact; $R^i\Gamma$ measures exactly the right-exactness defect via a long exact sequence.

questions.q: Which class of sheaves is NOT automatically $\Gamma$-acyclic?
choices: soft sheaves on a paracompact Hausdorff space
questions.explain: Injective, flabby, and soft (on paracompact Hausdorff) sheaves are all $\Gamma$-acyclic. A locally constant sheaf like $\underline{\mathbb{Z}}$ on $S^1$ has nonzero $H^1$, so it is not acyclic in general.

questions.q: On $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ the line bundle $\mathcal{O}(-5)$ has $h^0=0$. Using $\dim_k H^1(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}(n))=\max(-n-1,0)$, compute $\dim_k H^1(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}(-5))$.
The Picard group of a ringed space $(X,\mathcal{O}_X)$ is canonically isomorphic to which Čech cohomology group?
questions.explain: Line bundles are classified by their transition cocycles in $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$ modulo coboundaries: $\mathrm{Pic}(X)\cong\check{H}^1(X,\mathcal{O}_X^\times)$.

questions.q: On $\mathbb{P}^1$ with $\omega_{\mathbb{P}^1}=\mathcal{O}(-2)$, Serre duality gives $\dim H^1(\mathcal{O}(-7))=\dim H^0(\mathcal{O}(7-2))$. Compute this common dimension.
questions.explain: $\dim H^0(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}(5))=5+1=6$, and $\max(-(-7)-1,0)=6$, so the duality check holds.

questions.q: Use Riemann–Roch on a smooth projective curve of genus $g=4$ together with Serre duality to compute $\dim H^0(C,L)-\dim H^0(C,\omega_C\otimes L^{-1})$ for a line bundle $L$ of degree $d=10$.
questions.explain: Riemann–Roch: $h^0(L)-h^1(L)=d-g+1=10-4+1=7$, and Serre duality identifies $h^1(L)=h^0(\omega_C\otimes L^{-1})$.
